title: Mexican Chicken Tacos

subs.name: paneer or soya chunks for a vegetarian version
subs.notes: marinate the same way; reduce cook time to 4 minutes per side

subs.name: thin wheat roti or chapati works as an excellent wrap base
thick coconut yogurt for a dairy-free topping
whipped curd with a pinch of salt and lemon approximates the creamy element

steps.text: Combine red chilli powder, cumin, minced garlic, 1 tbsp lime juice, 1 tbsp oil and salt in a bowl. Add the chicken thighs and coat well on all sides. Set aside for at least 10 minutes to marinate.
Heat a tawa or heavy frying pan over high flame until very hot — a drop of water should evaporate immediately on contact. Add a thin film of oil and lay the chicken thighs flat without overlapping.

Cook without moving for 5 minutes until the underside is deeply browned with charred edges. Flip and cook the second side for another 4–5 minutes until the thighs are cooked through and the juices run clear when pierced at the thickest point.

Transfer the chicken to a board and rest for 3 minutes, then slice thinly across the grain. While the chicken rests, warm the tortillas one at a time on the same hot tawa, about 30 seconds per side until soft with light char spots.

Mix the diced onion, tomato and coriander with the remaining lime juice and a pinch of salt to make a quick pico. Build each taco: pile chicken on a tortilla, spoon on the pico, add avocado or curd if using, and serve immediately while the tortilla is still warm.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: chicken → paneer or soya chunks for a vegetarian version (marinate the same way; reduce cook time to 4 minutes per side); tortilla → thin wheat roti or chapati works as an excellent wrap base; avocado → whipped curd with a pinch of salt and lemon approximates the creamy element; curd → thick coconut yogurt for a dairy-free topping
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Cook the chicken on a heavy iron tawa or in a well-seasoned kadai on high flame to replicate the charred edges. Thin wheat rotis are the ideal tortilla substitute — roll them to roughly 6 inches before cooking. Use kashmiri chilli powder in place of smoked paprika for colour without too much heat. Hung curd or thick dahi is the best stand-in for sour cream and is readily available at any Indian dairy.
